NetSparkleUpdater.​SparkleUpdater
IDisposable
public
NetSparkleUpdater.​SparkleUpdater
NetSparkleUpdater.​Samples.​Avalonia.​CustomSparkleUpdater

Class to communicate with a sparkle-based appcast to download and install updates to an application

Variables
_installerProcess
protected Process? _installerProcess

The Process responsible for launching the downloaded update.  Only valid once the application is about to quit and the update is going to be launched.  Use `InstallerProcess` if you are a software app that updates other apps so you can monitor the installation process.

_shouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller
private bool _shouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller
_processIDToKillBeforeInstallerRuns
private string? _processIDToKillBeforeInstallerRuns
_logWriter
private ILogger? _logWriter
_taskWorker
private readonly Task _taskWorker
_cancelToken
private CancellationToken _cancelToken
_cancelTokenSource
private CancellationTokenSource _cancelTokenSource
_appReferenceAssembly
private readonly string? _appReferenceAssembly
_doInitialCheck
private bool _doInitialCheck
_forceInitialCheck
private bool _forceInitialCheck
_exitHandle
private readonly EventWaitHandle _exitHandle
_loopingHandle
private readonly EventWaitHandle _loopingHandle
_checkFrequency
private TimeSpan _checkFrequency
_tmpDownloadFilePath
private string? _tmpDownloadFilePath
_tmpDownloadFileNameWithExtension
private string? _tmpDownloadFileNameWithExtension
_downloadTempFileName
private string? _downloadTempFileName
_itemBeingDownloaded
private AppCastItem? _itemBeingDownloaded
_hasAttemptedFileRedownload
private bool _hasAttemptedFileRedownload
_latestDownloadedUpdateInfo
private UpdateInfo? _latestDownloadedUpdateInfo
_uiFactory
private IUIFactory? _uiFactory
_disposed
private bool _disposed
_configuration
private Configuration? _configuration
_restartExecutableName
private string? _restartExecutableName
_restartExecutablePath
private string? _restartExecutablePath
_appCastHelper
private AppCastHelper? _appCastHelper
_appCastGenerator
private IAppCastGenerator? _appCastGenerator
_updateDownloader
private IUpdateDownloader? _updateDownloader
_actionToRunOnProgressWindowShown
private Action? _actionToRunOnProgressWindowShown

The progress window is shown on a separate thread.  In order to ensure that the download starts after the progress window is shown, we create an Action to run after things are ready.  It would be better if things ran using async/await, but this will suffice as a "fix" for now.

_syncContextForWorkerLoop
private SynchronizationContext _syncContextForWorkerLoop
Functions
SparkleUpdater
public SparkleUpdater(
string 
appcastUrl,
ISignatureVerifier 
signatureVerifier
)

Constructor which requires the app cast url and the object that will verify app cast signatures

Parameters
appcastUrl
string

the URL of the app cast file

signatureVerifier
ISignatureVerifier

the object that will verify your app cast signatures.

SparkleUpdater
public SparkleUpdater(
string 
appcastUrl,
ISignatureVerifier 
signatureVerifier,
string? 
referenceAssembly
)

ctor which needs the app cast url, an object to verify app cast signatures, and a reference assembly

Parameters
appcastUrl
string

the URL of the app cast file

signatureVerifier
ISignatureVerifier

the object that will verify your app cast signatures.

referenceAssembly
string?

the name of the assembly to use for comparison when checking update versions

SparkleUpdater
public SparkleUpdater(
string 
appcastUrl,
ISignatureVerifier 
signatureVerifier,
string? 
referenceAssembly,
IUIFactory? 
factory
)

Constructor that performs all necessary initialization for software update checking

Parameters
appcastUrl
string

the URL of the app cast file

signatureVerifier
ISignatureVerifier

the object that will verify your app cast signatures.

referenceAssembly
string?

the name of the assembly to use for comparison when checking update versions

factory
IUIFactory?

a UI factory to use in place of the default UI

Properties
SecurityProtocolType
[Obsolete("Deprecated in .NET 9; this property may be removed at any time " + "(including minor/patch updates to this library); override pertinent " + "functions (e.g. to HttpClient) in WebRequestAppCastDataDownloader or " + "similar to make equivalent changes to your application")]
public SecurityProtocolType SecurityProtocolType {
get;
set
}

The security protocol used by NetSparkle. Setting this property will also set this for the current AppDomain of the caller. May need to be set to SecurityProtocolType.Tls12 for some cases (such as when downloading from GitHub); however, if your app is using a more recent version of .NET, you should be OK.  See also: https://​stackoverflow​.com​/a​/59398678​/3938401 .NET 9 -> ServicePointManager is deprecated

UserInteractionMode
public UserInteractionMode UserInteractionMode {
get;
set
}

Set the user interaction mode for Sparkle to use when there is a valid update for the software

TmpDownloadFilePath
public string? TmpDownloadFilePath {
get;
set
}

If set, downloads files to this path. If the folder doesn't already exist, creates the folder at download time (and not before).  Note that this variable is a path, NOT a full file name.  By default, the file name is grabbed from the server and/or download link.  You can use  to control the actual file name (NOT path) if you wish.

TmpDownloadFileNameWithExtension
public string? TmpDownloadFileNameWithExtension {
get;
set
}

If set, downloads files to this file name. Note that this variable is a file name, not a path, and it should not be treated as a path. Use  to control the download path.  If set, overrides any file name / extension grabbing from the server.  You probably want to set this manually before the item is downloaded to have some unique filename + extension based on the item that's going to be downloaded, or a UUID with extension, or similar.

RelaunchAfterUpdate
public bool RelaunchAfterUpdate {
get;
set
}

Defines if the application needs to be relaunched after executing the downloaded installer

CustomInstallerArguments
public string? CustomInstallerArguments {
get;
set
}

Run the downloaded installer with these arguments

ClearOldInstallers
public Action? ClearOldInstallers {
get;
set
}

Function that is called asynchronously to clean up old installers that have been downloaded with SilentModeTypes.DownloadNoInstall or SilentModeTypes.DownloadAndInstall.

IsUpdateLoopRunning
public bool IsUpdateLoopRunning {
get
}

Whether or not the update loop is running

UIFactory
public IUIFactory? UIFactory {
get;
set
}

Factory for creating UI elements like progress window, etc.

UpdateAvailableWindow
private IUpdateAvailable? UpdateAvailableWindow {
get;
set
}

The user interface that shows the release notes and asks the user to skip, remind me later, or update

ProgressWindow
private IDownloadProgress? ProgressWindow {
get;
set
}

The user interface that shows a download progress bar, and then asks to install and relaunch the application

CheckingForUpdatesWindow
private ICheckingForUpdates? CheckingForUpdatesWindow {
get;
set
}

The user interface that shows the 'Checking for Updates...' UIrm.

Configuration
public Configuration Configuration {
get;
set
}

The configuration object for a given assembly that has information on when updates were checked last, any updates that have been skipped, etc.

RestartExecutablePath
public string RestartExecutablePath {
get;
set
}

Path to the working directory for the current application.  This is the directory that the current executable sits in -- e.g. C:/Users/...Foo/. It will be used when restarting the application on Windows or will be used on macOS/Linux for overwriting files on an update.

RestartExecutableName
public string RestartExecutableName {
get;
set
}

Executable name to use when restarting the software.  This is the name that will be used/started when the update has been installed.  This defaults to Environment.CommandLine.  Used in conjunction with RestartExecutablePath to restart the application -- cd "{RestartExecutablePath}" {RelaunchAfterUpdateCommandPrefix}"{RestartExecutableName}" is what is called to restart the app, so make sure you add a space after RelaunchAfterUpdateCommandPrefix if needed.

RelaunchAfterUpdateCommandPrefix
public string? RelaunchAfterUpdateCommandPrefix {
get;
set
}

Defines a command prefix that will be used to open the executable (like "dotnet " or "./" on Linux systems).  This prefix command will not be auto-escaped with quotes for you, so if that's needed, make sure to do it yourself.  Contribution from @daniel-pastalab

SignatureVerifier
public ISignatureVerifier SignatureVerifier {
get;
set
}

The object that verifies signatures (DSA, Ed25519, or otherwise) of downloaded items

AppCastUrl
public string AppCastUrl {
get;
set
}

Gets or sets the app cast URL

UseNotificationToast
public bool UseNotificationToast {
get;
set
}

Specifies if you want to use the notification toast message (not implemented in all UIs).

LogWriter
public ILogger? LogWriter {
get;
set
}

Object that handles any diagnostic messages for NetSparkle.  If you want to use your own class for this, you should just need to override LogWriter.PrintMessage in your own class.  Make sure to set this object before calling StartLoop(bool) to guarantee that all messages will get sent to the right place!

CheckServerFileName
public bool CheckServerFileName {
get;
set
}

Whether or not to check with the online server to verify download file names.

LatestAppCastItems
public List<AppCastItem>? LatestAppCastItems {
get
}

Returns the latest appcast items to the caller. Might be null.

UpdateMarkedCritical
public bool UpdateMarkedCritical {
get
}

Loops through all of the most recently grabbed app cast items and checks if any of them are marked as critical

UpdateDownloader
public IUpdateDownloader UpdateDownloader {
get;
set
}

The object responsable for downloading update files for your application

AppCastDataDownloader
public IAppCastDataDownloader? AppCastDataDownloader {
get;
set
}

The object responsible for downloading app cast and app cast signature information for your application

AppCastHelper
public AppCastHelper AppCastHelper {
get;
set
}

The object responsible for parsing app cast information and checking to see if any updates are available in a given app cast

AppCastGenerator
public IAppCastGenerator AppCastGenerator {
get;
set
}

Object responsible for serializing and deserializing  objects after data has been downloaded.

ShouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller
public bool ShouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller {
get;
set
}

When running the downloaded installer/update file, before installing it, whether or not to kill the parent process that initiated the software update process (generally the thing that is controlling the SparkleUpdater instance).  Defaults to true. Set to false if for some reason you don't want your app to restart or die when the installer starts (e.g. if it is an optional update or an update for something outside of the software itself).

ProcessIDToKillBeforeInstallerRuns
public string ProcessIDToKillBeforeInstallerRuns {
get;
set
}

The process ID that should be killed before the installer runs. This is nullable.  On starting the installer/updater file, if this property is null/whitespace, the process that will be killed is Process.GetCurrentProcess().Id.ToString(), unless ShouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller is set to false.  Does not matter what you set on this property if ShouldKillParentProcessWhenStartingInstaller is false.

InstallerProcess
public Process? InstallerProcess {
get
}

The Process responsible for launching the downloaded update.  Only valid once the application is about to quit and the update is going to be launched.  Use this if you are a software app that updates other apps so you can monitor the installation process. Otherwise, if you're just running an installer for the software that's running, this will not be very helpful to use.

AppCastCache
public AppCast? AppCastCache {
get;
set
}

A cache / copy of the most recently downloaded .  Set after parsing an app cast that was downloaded from online.  This property only for convenience's sake.  Use if you aren't storing the app cast downloaded data yourself somewhere.  Will be wiped/reset/changed after downloading a new app cast, so be careful using this if you aren't saving the data yourself somewhere.

Functions
StartLoop
public async Task StartLoop(
bool 
doInitialCheck
)

Starts a SparkleUpdater background loop to check for updates every 24 hours.

You should only call this function when your app is initialized and shows its main UI.

Parameter
doInitialCheck
bool

whether the first check should happen before or after the first interval

StartLoop
public async Task StartLoop(
bool 
doInitialCheck,
TimeSpan 
checkFrequency
)

Starts a SparkleUpdater background loop to check for updates on a given interval.

You should only call this function when your app is initialized and shows its main UI.

Parameters
doInitialCheck
bool

whether the first check should happen before or after the first interval

checkFrequency
TimeSpan

the interval to wait between update checks

StartLoop
public async Task StartLoop(
bool 
doInitialCheck,
bool 
forceInitialCheck
)

Starts a SparkleUpdater background loop to check for updates every 24 hours.  Loop pauses for a few seconds on first run so that if the loop is started with the app, the user isn't immediately shown an update window.

You should only call this function when your app is initialized and shows its main UI.

Parameters
doInitialCheck
bool

whether the first check should happen before or after the first interval

forceInitialCheck
bool

if doInitialCheck is true, whether the first check should happen even if the last check was less than 24 hours ago

StartLoop
public async Task StartLoop(
bool 
doInitialCheck,
bool 
forceInitialCheck,
TimeSpan 
checkFrequency
)

Starts a SparkleUpdater background loop to check for updates on a given interval.  Loop pauses for a few seconds on first run so that if the loop is started with the app, the user isn't immediately shown an update window.

You should only call this function when your app is initialized and shows its main UIw.

Parameters
doInitialCheck
bool

whether the first check should happen before or after the first interval

forceInitialCheck
bool

if doInitialCheck is true, whether the first check should happen even if the last check was within the last checkFrequency interval

checkFrequency
TimeSpan

the interval to wait between update checks

StopLoop
public void StopLoop()

Stops the SparkleUpdater background loop. Called automatically by Dispose().

~SparkleUpdater
~SparkleUpdater()

Finalizer

Dispose
public void Dispose()

Inherited from IDisposable. Stops all background activities.

Dispose
protected virtual void Dispose(
bool 
disposing
)

Dispose of managed and unmanaged resources

Parameter
disposing
bool

true if the object is currently being disposed; false otherwise

UnregisterEvents
private void UnregisterEvents()

Unregisters events so that we don't call events more often than we should

GetUpdateStatus
protected async Task<UpdateInfo> GetUpdateStatus(
Configuration 
config,
bool 
ignoreSkippedVersions
 = 
false
)

This method checks if an update is required. During this process the appcast will be downloaded and checked against the reference assembly. Ensure that the calling process has read access to the reference assembly.  This method is also called from the background loops.

Parameters
config
Configuration

the SparkleUpdater configuration for the reference assembly

ignoreSkippedVersions
bool

true to ignore skipped versions, false otherwise

Returns

UpdateInfo with information on whether there is an update available or not.

ShowUpdateNeededUI
public void ShowUpdateNeededUI(
List<AppCastItem>? 
updates,
bool 
isUpdateAlreadyDownloaded
 = 
false
)

Shows the update needed UI with the given set of updates. Shows nothing if the number of updates is 0.

Parameters
updates
List<AppCastItem>?

updates to show UI for

isUpdateAlreadyDownloaded
bool

If true, make sure UI text shows that the user is about to install the file instead of download it.

ShowUpdateNeededUI
public void ShowUpdateNeededUI(
bool 
isUpdateAlreadyDownloaded
 = 
false
)

Shows the update UI with the latest downloaded update information.

Parameter
isUpdateAlreadyDownloaded
bool

If true, make sure UI text shows that the user is about to install the file instead of download it.

OnToastClick
private void OnToastClick(
List<AppCastItem> 
updates
)
ShowUpdateAvailableWindow
private void ShowUpdateAvailableWindow(
List<AppCastItem> 
updates,
bool 
isUpdateAlreadyDownloaded
 = 
false
)
GetDownloadPathForAppCastItem
public virtual async Task<string?> GetDownloadPathForAppCastItem(
AppCastItem 
item
)

Get the download path for a given app cast item.  If any directories need to be created, this function will create those directories.

Parameter
item
AppCastItem

The item that you want to generate a download path for

Returns

The download path for an app cast item if item is not null and has valid download link Otherwise returns null.

InitAndBeginDownload
public async Task InitAndBeginDownload(
AppCastItem 
item
)

Starts the download process by grabbing the download path for the app cast item (asynchronous so that it can get the server's download name in case there is a redirect; cancel this by setting CheckServerFileName to false), then beginning the download process if the download file doesn't already exist

Parameter
item
AppCastItem

the appcast item to download

OnDownloadStarted
private void OnDownloadStarted(
object 
sender,
string 
from,
string 
to
)
OnDownloadProgressChanged
private void OnDownloadProgressChanged(
object 
sender,
ItemDownloadProgressEventArgs 
args
)
CleanUpUpdateDownloader
private void CleanUpUpdateDownloader()
CreateAndShowProgressWindow
private void CreateAndShowProgressWindow(
AppCastItem 
castItem,
bool 
shouldShowAsDownloadedAlready
)
ProgressWindowCompleted
private async void ProgressWindowCompleted(
object 
sender,
DownloadInstallEventArgs 
args
)
OnDownloadFinished
private void OnDownloadFinished(
object? 
sender,
AsyncCompletedEventArgs 
e
)

Called when the installer is downloaded

Parameters
sender
object?

the object that initiated this event call

e
AsyncCompletedEventArgs

information on if the download was successful.

InstallUpdate
public async Task InstallUpdate(
AppCastItem 
item,
string? 
installPath
 = 
null
)

Run the provided app cast item update regardless of what else is going on.  Note that a more up to date download may be taking place, so if you don't want to run a potentially out-of-date installer, don't use this. This should only be used if your user wants to update before another update has been installed AND the file is already downloaded.  This function will verify that the file exists and that the signature is valid before running. It will also utilize the PreparingToExit event to ensure that the application can close.

Parameters
item
AppCastItem

AppCastItem to install

installPath
string?

Install path to the executable. If not provided, will ask the server for the download path.

IsDownloadingItem
public bool IsDownloadingItem(
AppCastItem 
item
)

Checks to see if the item passed into this function is the one being downloaded

Parameter
item
AppCastItem

to check

Returns

true if this item is currently being downloaded; false otherwise

IsDownloadingSilently
private bool IsDownloadingSilently()

True if the user has silent updates enabled; false otherwise.

DoExtensionsMatch
protected bool DoExtensionsMatch(
string 
extension,
string 
otherExtension
)

Checks to see if two extensions match (this is basically just a convenient string comparison). Both extensions should include the initial . (full-stop/period) in the extension.

Parameters
extension
string

first extension to check

otherExtension
string

other extension to check

Returns

true if the extensions match; false otherwise

GetWindowsInstallerCommand
protected virtual string GetWindowsInstallerCommand(
string 
downloadFilePath
)

Get the install command for the file at the given path. Figures out which command to use based on the download file path's file extension.  Currently supports .exe, .msi, and .msp.

Parameter
downloadFilePath
string

Path to the downloaded update file

Returns

the installer command if the file has one of the given extensions; the initial downloadFilePath if not.

GetInstallerCommand
protected virtual string GetInstallerCommand(
string 
downloadFilePath
)

Get the install command for the file at the given path. Figures out which command to use based on the download file path's file extension.

Windows: currently supports .exe, .msi, and .msp.

macOS: currently supports .tar, .tar.gz, .pkg, .dmg, and .zip.

Linux: currently supports .tar.gz, .deb, and .rpm.

Parameter
downloadFilePath
string

Path to the downloaded update file

Returns

the installer command if the file has one of the given extensions; the initial downloadFilePath if not.

IsZipDownload
protected bool IsZipDownload(
string 
downloadFilePath
)

Checks to see if the file at the path is a zip download.  If user is on macOS and extension is a .tar/.tar.gz/.zip, returns true.  If user is on Linux and extension is a .tar.gz, returns true.  Otherwise returns false. Always returns false on .NET Framework.

Parameter
downloadFilePath
string

Path to the downloaded update file

Returns

True if on macOS and path is a .zip, true of on Linux and path is a .tar.gz. False otherwise.

RunDownloadedInstaller
protected virtual async Task RunDownloadedInstaller(
string 
downloadFilePath
)

Updates the application via the file at the given path. Figures out which command needs to be run, sets up the application so that it will start the downloaded file once the main application stops, and then waits to start the downloaded update.

Parameter
downloadFilePath
string

path to the downloaded installer/updater

Returns

the awaitable Task for the application quitting

Exec
protected bool Exec(
string 
cmd,
bool 
waitForExit
 = 
true,
string 
downloadFilePath
 = 
""
)

Execute a shell script.

https://​stackoverflow​.com​/a​/47918132​/3938401

Parameters
cmd
string

Path to script to run via a shell

waitForExit
bool

True for the calling process to wait for the command to finish before exiting; false otherwise

downloadFilePath
string

Optional param for download file that is being executed via installer

Returns

true if process started, false if user canceled installer via InstallerProcessAboutToStart

QuitApplication
public async Task QuitApplication()

Quits the application (host application) that is using/started SparkleUpdater

Returns

Asynchronous task that can be awaited to call code after the application is quit (which may or may not be worth it based on your program setup)

AskApplicationToSafelyCloseUp
private async Task<bool> AskApplicationToSafelyCloseUp()

Ask the application to close their current work items.  Apps may need, for example, to let the user save their work

Returns

true if it's OK to run the installer and close the software; false otherwise

CheckForUpdatesAtUserRequest
public async Task<UpdateInfo> CheckForUpdatesAtUserRequest(
bool 
ignoreSkippedVersions
 = 
false
)

Check for updates, using UI interaction appropriate for if the user initiated the update request true to ignore skipped versions, false otherwise

CheckingForUpdatesWindow_Closing
private void CheckingForUpdatesWindow_Closing(
object? 
sender,
EventArgs 
e
)
CheckForUpdatesQuietly
public async Task<UpdateInfo> CheckForUpdatesQuietly(
bool 
ignoreSkippedVersions
 = 
false
)

Check for updates, using interaction appropriate for where the user doesn't know you're doing it, so be polite.  Basically, this checks for updates without showing a UI. NO UI WILL BE SHOWN. You must handle any showing of the UI yourself -- see the "HandleEventsYourself" sample!  true to ignore skipped versions, false otherwise

CheckForUpdates
private async Task<UpdateInfo> CheckForUpdates(
bool 
isUserManuallyCheckingForUpdates,
bool 
ignoreSkippedVersions
= 
false
)

Perform a one-time check for updates true if user triggered the update check (so show UI), false otherwise (no UI) true to ignore skipped versions, false otherwise

CancelFileDownload
public void CancelFileDownload()

Cancels an in-progress download of an app cast file and deletes the temporary file.

OnUpdateWindowUserResponded
private async void OnUpdateWindowUserResponded(
object 
sender,
UpdateResponseEventArgs 
args
)
OnWorkerDoWork
private async void OnWorkerDoWork()

Loop that occasionally checks for updates for the running application

OnWorkerProgressChanged
private void OnWorkerProgressChanged(
object 
sender,
ProgressChangedEventArgs 
e
)

This method will be notified by the SparkleUpdater loop when some update info has been downloaded. If the info has been downloaded fully (e.ProgressPercentage == 1), the UI for downloading updates will be shown (if not downloading silently) or the download will be performed (if downloading silently).

UpdatesHaveBeenDownloaded
private async void UpdatesHaveBeenDownloaded(
List<AppCastItem>? 
updates
)

Updates from appcast have been downloaded from the server.  If the user is downloading silently, the download will begin.  If the user is not downloading silently, the update UI will be shown.

Parameter
updates
List<AppCastItem>?

Updates to be installed. If null, nothing will happen.

Events
LoopStarted
public event LoopStartedOperation? LoopStarted

This event will be raised when an update check is about to be started

LoopFinished
public event LoopFinishedOperation? LoopFinished

This event will be raised when an update check has finished

UpdateCheckStarted
public event UpdateCheckStarted? UpdateCheckStarted

Called when update check has just begun

UpdateDetected
public event UpdateDetected? UpdateDetected

This event can be used to override the standard user interface process when an update is detected. To modify the next action, change the value of the property.  If both this and UpdateDetectedAsync are implemented, UpdateDetectedAsync is used, and UpdateDetected is not called.

UpdateDetectedAsync
public event UpdateDetectedAsync? UpdateDetectedAsync

This event can be used to override the standard user interface process when an update is detected. To modify the next action, change the value of the property.  If both this and UpdateDetected are implemented, UpdateDetectedAsync is used, and UpdateDetected is not called.

UpdateCheckFinished
public event UpdateCheckFinished? UpdateCheckFinished

Called when update check is all done. UpdateDetected may have been called between the start and end of the update check.

UserRespondedToUpdate
public event UserRespondedToUpdate? UserRespondedToUpdate

The user responded to the update UI with a given response.  This is called _after_ NetSparkle handles the response in all cases EXCEPT when the result is UpdateAvailableResult.InstallUpdate -- in that case, this event is called BEFORE downloading the update begins.

DownloadStarted
public event DownloadEvent? DownloadStarted

Called when the download of an app cast file has just started

DownloadCanceled
public event DownloadEvent? DownloadCanceled

Called when the download of an app cast file has been canceled

DownloadHadError
public event DownloadErrorEvent? DownloadHadError

Called when the download of an app cast file has downloaded but has an error other than corruption

DownloadMadeProgress
public event ItemDownloadProgressEvent? DownloadMadeProgress

Called when the download of an app cast file has made some progress.  This data is also sent to the progress window if one is available.

DownloadFinished
public event DownloadEvent? DownloadFinished

Called when the downloaded file is fully downloaded and verified regardless of the value for SilentMode. Note that if you are installing fully silently, this will be called before the install file is executed, so don't manually initiate the file or anything. Useful when using SilentModeTypes.DownloadNoInstall so you can let your user know when the downloaded update is ready.

DownloadedFileIsCorrupt
public event DownloadEvent? DownloadedFileIsCorrupt

Called when the downloaded file is already downloaded (or at least partially on disk) and the DSA signature doesn't match. When this is called, Sparkle is not taking any further action to try to download the install file during this instance of the software. In order to make Sparkle try again, you must delete the file off disk yourself. Sparkle will try again after the software is restarted. This event could allow you to tell the user what happened if updates are silent.

DownloadedFileThrewWhileCheckingSignature
public event DownloadEvent? DownloadedFileThrewWhileCheckingSignature

Called when the downloaded file is fully downloaded and has its signature checked, but something went wrong during the signature check.  If you're sure the file is there, you can try verifying the file again AND triggering an install with SparkleUpdater.InstallUpdate, but make sure to warn your users first!

InstallUpdateFailed
public event InstallUpdateFailure? InstallUpdateFailed

Called when InstallUpdate fails for one reason or another.

PreparingToExit
public event CancelEventHandler? PreparingToExit

Subscribe to this event to get a chance to shut down gracefully before the application is closed.  If PreparingToExitAsync is set, this has no effect.

PreparingToExitAsync
public event CancelEventHandlerAsync? PreparingToExitAsync

Subscribe to this event to get a chance to asynchronously shut down gracefully before the application is closed.  This overrides PreparingToExit.

CloseApplication
public event CloseApplication? CloseApplication

Event for custom shutdown logic. If this is set, it is called instead of Application.Current.Shutdown or Application.Exit.  If CloseApplicationAsync is set, this has no effect.

Warning: The script that launches your executable only waits for 90 seconds before giving up! Make sure that your software closes within 90 seconds if you implement this event!  If you need an event that can be canceled, use PreparingToExit.

CloseApplicationAsync
public event CloseApplicationAsync? CloseApplicationAsync

Event for asynchronous custom shutdown logic. If this is set, it is called instead of Application.Current.Shutdown or Application.Exit.  This overrides CloseApplication.

Warning: The script that launches your executable only waits for 90 seconds before giving up! Make sure that your software closes within 90 seconds if you implement this event!  If you need an event that can be canceled, use PreparingToExitAsync.

InstallerProcessAboutToStart
public event BeforeBeginInstallerProcess? InstallerProcessAboutToStart

Event to modify/see the installer process before it actually begins. You can set properties on the Process before it by using this event.  Return true to keep the installer process going, return false to have SparkleUpdater not run the installer (you can choose not to run it at all or run it yourself after that).
